title: The keychain on my keys
summary: Describe the keychain on your keys — what it looks like and why you keep it.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a keychain on your keys. Describe what it looks like, where it came from, and why you keep it. Use present simple.
hintStarters: I have a keychain on my keys. It is…
sampleText: I have a small keychain attached to my flat keys. It is a tiny rubber figure of a penguin. The penguin is black and white and about three centimetres tall. I got it at a gift shop in Cape Town when I visited South Africa three years ago. I put it on my keys the same day and I never took it off. The penguin's colour is still good, but one of its wings is a little worn out. Every time I take my keys out of my bag, I see the penguin. It reminds me of the trip and of the beach where I saw real penguins for the first time. My friends sometimes ask me about the keychain. I always tell them the story. Some people swap keychains often, but I do not want to change mine. It is small and simple, but it is one of my favourite things. I will keep it for a long time.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: gắn vào chùm chìa khóa căn hộ

sampleAnnotations.phrase: tiny rubber figure of a penguin
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: hình chim cánh cụt cao su nhỏ xíu

sampleAnnotations.phrase: take my keys out of my bag
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: lấy chìa khóa ra khỏi túi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: keep it for a long time

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed events
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để nói về những việc đã xảy ra và kết thúc: 'I got it in Cape Town', 'I visited South Africa three years ago', 'I saw real penguins for the first time'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the past simple for finished events: 'I got it in Cape Town', 'I visited South Africa three years ago', 'I saw real penguins for the first time'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for current states
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để mô tả trạng thái hiện tại: 'It is a tiny rubber figure', 'The penguin is black and white', 'It reminds me of the trip'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple to describe current states: 'It is a tiny rubber figure', 'The penguin is black and white', 'It reminds me of the trip'.

sampleTranslationVi: Tôi có một móc chìa khóa nhỏ gắn vào chùm chìa khóa căn hộ. Đó là hình chim cánh cụt cao su nhỏ xíu. Chim cánh cụt màu đen trắng và cao khoảng ba xăng-ti-mét. Tôi mua nó ở một cửa hàng quà lưu niệm ở Cape Town khi thăm Nam Phi ba năm trước. Tôi móc nó vào chùm chìa khóa ngay ngày hôm đó và chưa bao giờ tháo ra. Màu sắc của chim cánh cụt vẫn còn tốt nhưng một cánh của nó hơi cũ sờn. Mỗi lần lấy chìa khóa ra khỏi túi, tôi thấy con chim cánh cụt. Nó gợi nhớ về chuyến đi và bãi biển nơi tôi lần đầu tiên nhìn thấy chim cánh cụt thật. Bạn bè đôi khi hỏi tôi về móc chìa khóa. Tôi luôn kể câu chuyện. Một số người thường xuyên đổi móc chìa khóa nhưng tôi không muốn thay cái của mình. Nó nhỏ và đơn giản nhưng là một trong những thứ tôi yêu thích nhất. Tôi sẽ giữ nó lâu dài.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the keychain look like?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'It is a tiny rubber figure of a penguin'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the sentence that describes what the keychain is.

mcQuestions.prompt: Where did the writer get the keychain?
options: At a gift shop in Cape Town
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I got it at a gift shop in Cape Town'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the city and type of shop mentioned.

mcQuestions.prompt: How tall is the penguin keychain?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'about three centimetres tall'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the measurement in the description.

mcQuestions.prompt: What has happened to the keychain over time?
options: One wing is a little worn out
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'one of its wings is a little worn out'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the small damage mentioned in the description.

mcQuestions.prompt: What does the keychain remind the writer of?
options: The trip and the beach where they saw real penguins
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'It reminds me of the trip and of the beach where I saw real penguins'.
mcQuestions.hint: Find what memory the keychain brings back.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which past simple form is correct?
options: I getted it in Cape Town.
I get it in Cape Town.
I got it in Cape Town.
I am getting it in Cape Town.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'get' is irregular — its past simple form is 'got': 'I got it in Cape Town'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the irregular past simple of 'get'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Has the writer ever taken the penguin off the keys?
options: Yes, once when washing the keys
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'I put it on my keys the same day and I never took it off'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the word 'never' in the text.

mcQuestions.prompt: What do some people do with keychains that the writer does not?
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'Some people swap keychains often, but I do not want to change mine'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the contrast between the writer and 'some people'.

options: It reminds of me the trip.
It remind me of the trip.
It reminds me of the trip.
It is remind me of the trip.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'remind someone of something' is the correct structure: 'It reminds me of the trip'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the structure: remind + object + of + something.

mcQuestions.prompt: How does the writer feel about the keychain?
options: Fond of it and plans to keep it
Not sure why they still have it
mcQuestions.explanation: The text says 'it is one of my favourite things. I will keep it for a long time'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the feelings and intention expressed at the end.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small objects like souvenirs often carry big memories? What makes something worth keeping for a long time?
